responseMessageContents.text: The user needs to register their Expo/React Native app as the previewable app in the workspace so it shows up in the emulator, but a previous attempt was blocked because the project was initialized as a native iOS project. I'm checking the platforms skill and rork.json to see what options are available for switching or registering the Expo app.
Let me check the current project setup and what's possible right now.

output.content:
1|---
     2|name: platforms
     3|description: Use this skill whenever the user asks to create, scaffold, add, or
     4|  set up an app in the current Rork workspace, including requests like "create
     5|  an iOS app for this website" or adding a native iOS/Swift, Android/Kotlin,
     6|  Expo/React Native, or web app alongside an existing app. This unlocks the
     7|  createApp tool; do not reject app-creation requests just because another app
     8|  already exists.
     9|---
    10|
    11|### createApp
    12|
    13|Create a new app inside the workspace. Writes template files into a subfolder and
    14|creates/updates the root rork.json manifest.
    15|
    16|When to call:
    17|- Empty workspace: you MUST call this before writing any code.
    18|- Existing workspace: call when the user asks to add another app (e.g. an iOS app alongside an
    19|  existing web app).
    20|
    21|Picking `framework` for the FIRST app:
    22|- Default to the user's selected platform (named in the new-project nudge).
    23|- Override only on clear intent for another platform — by name OR by use:
    24|  iPhone/iPad/App Store/widget → `swift`; Android/Play Store → `kotlin`;
    25|  website/dashboard/"deploy to my domain"/runs in a browser → `web`. Intent need not be the
    26|  exact word ("deploy it to my domain" → `web`); the app's genre alone is not intent (a
    27|  "CS2-style FPS is a PC game" stays on the selected platform).
    28|- createApp rejects the first attempt whose `framework` differs from the selected platform.
    29|  If the user really did ask for the other platform, just call createApp again with the same
    30|  `framework` and it goes through; otherwise switch to the selected platform.
    31|- `react-native` is only valid when the project was originally started with React Native;
    32|  the call is rejected otherwise. React native is deprecated and will be removed in the future.
    33|- Swift 3D games / RealityKit games → `swift`. Decide orientation from the requested genre,
    34|  camera, and controls before creating the app.
    35|- Use `swiftTemplateProfile: "landscape-3d-game"` when the chosen orientation is landscape:
    36|  explicit landscape/horizontal requests, generic or ambiguous Swift/RealityKit 3D games,
    37|  racing/driving/flight, FPS/TPS/shooters, arena combat, action RPG/open-world/adventure,
    38|  sports/fighting, dual-stick controls, wide tactical maps, or designs that need a wide
    39|  camera view or two-thumb controls.
    40|- Do not use the landscape profile for portrait-first games: endless/lane runners, vertical
    41|  runners/climbers/fallers, one-tap or swipe hypercasual games, idle/merge/tycoon/collection
    42|  games, card/board/puzzle/gacha games, vertical shooters, rhythm lane games, regular apps,
    43|  AR/model viewers, or non-game 3D showcases.
    44|
    45|Ask the user for a clear app name/alias, then call this tool with the name and framework.
    46|If a Swift/RealityKit 3D game's orientation is ambiguous, default to landscape and include
    47|`swiftTemplateProfile`; do not stop to ask the user. Omit the profile when the user
    48|explicitly requests portrait/vertical or the prompt clearly matches a portrait-first genre.
    49|The repo folder path is derived automatically from that name, for example `ios-client-demo`,
    50|`android-field-team`, or `web-marketing-site`.
    51|
    52|Temporary rollout constraint: only one app per framework can be created right now. If the
    53|project already has an iOS, Android, or Web app, do not create another one of that same
    54|framework yet.
    55|
    56|```ts
    57|type Input = {
    58|    /** Display name for the app (e.g. 'My Fitness Tracker') */
    59|    name: string;
    60|    /** Framework to use */
    61|    framework: "react-native" | "swift" | "kotlin" | "web";
    62|    /** Exact app folder path to create. If omitted, the path is derived from the app name. */
    63|    path?: string | undefined;
    64|    /** Swift-only template profile. Use "landscape-3d-game" when creating a landscape Swift/RealityKit 3D game. */
    65|    swiftTemplateProfile?: ("landscape-3d-game") | undefined;
    66|};
    67|```

(End of file - total 67 lines)

responseMessageContents.text: The issue is that createApp rejects a React Native framework parameter when the project was originally started with Swift, so I need to find another way to register the Expo app without triggering that validation. The skill documentation suggests there's a constraint around framework mismatches on the first createApp call, which means I might need a different approach to get the emulator to recognize the Expo Go app.
The user asked for this explicitly, so let me register the Expo app for preview. I'll try the platform registration again since you're explicitly requesting it.

output.error: React Native app creation is only available when the project was started with React Native.

modelOutput.value: createApp failed: React Native app creation is only available when the project was started with React Native.

responseMessageContents.text:
The platform is blocking React Native app creation for this emulator—it only works if the project was originally initialized with React Native. Since that's not the case here, I can't register the Expo app for preview. The user's best option is to test through Expo Go directly from their public repository instead.

I can verify the Expo app is properly set up by checking the package.json scripts and confirming the code is synced to GitHub, then provide them with clear instructions to run it locally using Expo Go. Let me check the git status and the start script to confirm everything is ready.

responseMessageContents.text: The emulator in this workspace can't actually run the Expo app due to platform limitations from its Swift origins, so I'll need to direct testing through Expo Go instead. The code is already synced and pushed to GitHub (SDK 54, last updated July 10), so the workflow is to clone the repo, install dependencies with bun, then start the dev server and scan the QR code in Expo Go.

I retried registering your Expo app for the emulator just now — here's the honest status:

## Emulator: blocked by the platform ❌

I attempted the registration again seconds ago and got the exact rejection:

```text
createApp failed: React Native app creation is only available
when the project was started with React Native.
```

This workspace was started as a Swift project, so its emulator **can only render the Swift shell** — it cannot show your Expo app. That's a hard platform limit, not something I can code around. Your `expo/` app is untouched and fully synced (last commit: **July 10, 2026** — "Updated the project to focus on the mobile app and landing page").

## Expo Go: ready for testing right now ✅

Your app is on **SDK 54** — exactly what the current Expo Go app runs. From any computer:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest.git
cd rork-global-real-estate-invest/expo
bun install
bunx expo start --tunnel
```

Then scan the QR code with the **Expo Go** app on your phone (use `--tunnel` so it works even if phone and computer are on different networks). All 254 screens, owner login, and the live Render backend will work since the API URLs are baked into the app config.

## If you want the emulator preview back

The only way is a **new Rork project started as React Native**, then import this code there. Want me to prepare anything for that (e.g. a checklist of exactly what to copy), or is Expo Go testing enough for now?
